channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Biden will decide on US Steel acquisition after influential panel fails to reach consensus
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A powerful government panel on Monday failed to reach consensus on the possible national security risks of a nearly $15 billion proposed deal for Nippon Steel of Japan to purchase U.S. Steel, leaving the decision to President Joe Biden, who opposes the deal.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, MARC LEVY and ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden vetoes once-bipartisan effort to add 66 federal judgeships, citing 'hurried' House action
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Monday vetoed a once-bipartisan effort to add 66 federal district judgeships, saying “hurried action” by the House left important questions unanswered about the life-tenured positions.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden signs defense bill despite objections to ban on transgender health care for military children
item.description: President Joe Biden has signed the annual defense authorization bill into law despite his objections to language banning transgender health care for military children.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump rep says Biden move to commute sentences for most death row inmates a 'slap in the face' to victims
item.description: President-elect Biden has been criticized for commuting the death sentences for 37 federal inmates, despite their violent crimes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trial for suspect in second Trump assassination attempt delayed
item.description: Lawyers for the suspect, Ryan Routh, sought a delay of the scheduled Feb. 10 trial date, citing the large volume of evidence in the case.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Ethics releases Matt Gaetz report: 5 takeaways
item.description: The report — which spans 42 pages and includes several more in exhibits — found “substantial evidence” that Gaetz violated House rules and state and federal laws.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Judge rules Arkansas law allowing criminal charges against librarians is unconstitutional
item.description: A federal judge has struck down key parts of an Arkansas law that would have allowed criminal charges against librarians and booksellers for providing so-called “harmful” materials to minors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bill Clinton hospitalized with fever but in good spirits, spokesperson says
item.description: Former President Bill Clinton was admitted Monday to Georgetown University Medical Center in Washington after developing a fever.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bill Clinton is hospitalized with a fever but in good spirits, spokesperson says
item.description: Former President Bill Clinton was admitted Monday to MedStar Georgetown University Hospital in Washington after developing a fever.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Iran's weakened position could lead it to pursue nuclear weapon, Biden national security adviser warns
item.description: The White House is concerned that Iran’s weakened position will prompt the regime to pursue a nuclear weapon and is coordinating with the Trump team on this concern.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ethics releases Matt Gaetz report: 5 takeaways
item.description: The report — which spans 42 pages and includes several more in exhibits — found “substantial evidence” that Gaetz violated House rules, state and federal laws, outlining allegations of prostitution, statutory rape and illicit drug use, among other accusations.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans hammer Biden for federal death row reprieves ahead of leaving office
item.description: Republicans tore President Biden apart for unveiling his decision to commute the sentences of almost everyone on federal death row.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Many Americans have come to rely on Chinese-made drones. Now lawmakers want to ban them
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Russell Hedrick, a North Carolina farmer, flies drones to spray fertilizers on his corn, soybean and wheat fields at a fraction of what it would cost him to use a conventional ground spreader.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden commutes sentences of 37 federal death row prisoners
item.description: Opponents of the death penalty had urged President Joe Biden to take this step, given the number of executions that took place during President-elect Donald Trump's first term.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: White House made 'friendliest threat' over Biden's decline: DNC committee member
item.description: According to Lindy Li, the official told her, "You're one of the President's most loyal supporters. We know you're going to stay loyal.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: What Trump's decision to wade into spending fight tells us about the next 4 years
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After days of threats and demands, Donald Trump had little to show for it once lawmakers passed a budget deal in the early hours of Saturday, narrowly averting a pre-Christmas government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, STEPHEN GROVES, JILL COLVIN and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms 235th Biden judge, surpassing Trump’s record
item.description: The Senate confirmed 234 of Trump’s judicial nominees during his first four years in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez faces uncertain political future
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) is facing an uncertain future after a pivotal setback this week, when she lost her race to lead Democrats on the House Oversight and Accountability Committee. The 74-year-old Rep. Gerry Connolly’s (D-Va.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: Lara Trump says she's removing herself from consideration to be Florida senator
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Lara Trump, the daughter-in-law of President-elect Donald Trump, said Saturday that she's removing herself from consideration to be a Florida senator — ending speculation that she could replace Republican Sen.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden signs bill that averts a government shutdown and brings a close to days of Washington upheaval
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden signed a bill into law Saturday that averts a government shutdown, bringing a final close to days of upheaval after Congress approved a temporary funding plan just past the deadline and refused President-elect Donald Trump’s core debt demands in the package.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, FARNOUSH AMIRI and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Fani Willis disqualification may doom Trump’s Georgia case
item.description: Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis’s (D) disqualification from Georgia's election interference case against President-elect Trump and his allies could mark the beginning of the end of the prosecution.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Government shutdown is averted just after deadline as Congress rejects Trump's debt limit demands
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Facing a government shutdown deadline, the Senate rushed through final passage early Saturday of a bipartisan plan that would temporarily fund federal operations and disaster aid, dropping President-elect Donald Trump's demands for a debt limit increase into the new year.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, FARNOUSH AMIRI and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats say lines of communication open with GOP on funding, but no bill presented
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) told his party that lines of communication have reopened between his office and Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) to avert a government shutdown this weekend, members said.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Government funding bill clears Congress and heads to President Biden, averting a shutdown
item.description: The Senate has rushed through final passage of a bipartisan plan to fund federal operations and prevent a government shutdown. It now goes to President Joe Biden, who is expected to quickly sign it into law.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: What a potential government shutdown could mean for San Diego
item.description: The federal government is barreling towards a partial shutdown after GOP lawmakers in Congress backed away from a deal on a short-term spending bill struck with Democrats just days before the Friday deadline.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5
